tribal_rules.reason: memory leak in production under load

tribal_rules.rule: Payments API responses use tx_id field, not transactionId
tribal_rules.pr_title: Standardize transaction ID field to tx_id

tribal_rules.rule: Mobile checkout uses useCheckout hook, not raw fetch
tribal_rules.reason: consolidated state management and error handling
tribal_rules.pr_title: Extract checkout logic into useCheckout hook

tribal_rules.rule: Auth middleware runs before logging middleware

story_arcs: Six weeks ago: Rate-limit incident caused checkout downtime for 20 minutes. Marcus investigated and found express-rate-limit was leaking memory under load. He wrote lib/limiter.ts (redis-backed) as replacement. Team agreed to deprecate express-rate-limit across all services. (Establishes R1.)

Three weeks ago: Cross-team alignment meeting where Alex pushed for tx_id naming convention. The mobile app was using transactionId while payments-api used tx_id. Alex made the case for consistency. Jin agreed to update mobile app. (R2.)

Two months ago: Mobile team was having issues with checkout state management. Multiple components were duplicating fetch logic. Jin consolidated everything into useCheckout hook. Marcus reviewed and approved. (R3.)

Four months ago: Security review caught that logging middleware was running before auth middleware, causing unauthenticated requests to be logged. Sarah flagged this as a security issue and led the fix. (R4.)
Last week: Open question on #payments about whether to add idempotency keys to refund endpoint. Alex is for it, Marcus concerned about redis load. Discussion ongoing.
Two weeks ago: Incident where inventory service went down, affecting checkout. Sarah led postmortem. Result: better circuit breaker patterns needed.
Five weeks ago: Marcus optimized redis connection pooling. Noticed checkout latency improved by 15%.
Yesterday: Jin shared mobile checkout UX improvements in #random. Team celebrated 99.9% checkout success rate for the week.

company.name: Acme Robotics
company.description: A robotics company building autonomous delivery bots for urban last-mile delivery.
